title: Typed Tool Contracts, JSON Schema, Descriptions, Domains, and Versions · Retrieval Circuit
summary: A delayed engineering review reconstructs typed tool contracts, json schema, descriptions, domains, and versions from stakeholder, revision, runtime, model, interaction, call, result, state, approval, evaluation, trace, failure, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for least-capability typed tool catalog after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define one narrow tool purpose, effect class, caller, user benefit, authority, timeout, idempotency, and prohibited use.
Specify required properties, types, bounds, patterns, enums, nested limits, and additional-property policy with a supported schema subset.
Write tool and parameter descriptions that distinguish when to call, when not to call, required evidence, effects, and failure semantics.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-ai-agent-python cumulative safe evaluated AI agent portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python interaction, tool, policy, state, memory,
evaluation, telemetry, and recovery models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only.
Provider APIs, Gemini models, native google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic, HTTP, credentials,
host files, code execution, browsers, databases, telemetry backends, load, cost, privacy review,
and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentPolicy:
    stakeholder_task_id: str
    allowed_tool_names: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_steps: int = 12
    maximum_tool_calls: int = 8
    approval_required_for_writes: bool = True


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentEvidence:
    revision: str
    model_config_digest: str
    policy_digest: str
    evaluation_digest: str
    recovery_drill_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Safe evaluated Python AI agent portfolio ready"

stimulus.title: Tool Schema Version case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A review agent engineering team handles case 2860 during an accessible public-service intake assistant. Build deterministic Python evidence to design narrow typed tool contracts with purpose, descriptions, domains, required fields, rejection, and version identity; sensitive actions require a trustworthy user approval bound to exact arguments; then change model capability, API version, schema, or provider state and revalidate the adapter. Browser code uses pure bounded state machines and original fixed fixtures only. Real provider APIs, Gemini models, google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic native validation, HTTP, files, credentials, code execution, browsers, databases, telemetry backends, load, privacy review, and production effects require explicit controlled transfer gates. lines.label: risk
lines.text: Tool schemas can change in place because the model sees only the latest definition.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Version tool contracts and provide compatibility, migration, deprecation, and replay tests for changed parameters and results. Artifact under review: least-capability typed tool catalog.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Tool Schema Version, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim does not survive the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary.

For separate evidence from confidence, run one convenient fluent model response, omit stakeholder risk, revision, model and API identity, interaction and call correlation, tool schema and authority, state and resource budgets, approval, changed fixtures, injection, privacy, accessibility, evaluation, recovery, and controlled provider transfer, then accept plausible text as agent proof.

steps.title: Safe AI agents and Python Record what required effort · Review Tool Schema Version
steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Tool Schema Version: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A review agent engineering team handles case 4479 during a clean package, canary, and recovery drill. Build deterministic Python evidence to design narrow typed tool contracts with purpose, descriptions, domains, required fields, rejection, and version identity; model output and tool arguments remain untrusted until validated; then introduce a sensitive action, poisoned memory, leaking log, or computer-use request and deny or pause it. Browser code uses pure bounded state machines and original fixed fixtures only. Real provider APIs, Gemini models, google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic native validation, HTTP, files, credentials, code execution, browsers, databases, telemetry backends, load, privacy review, and production effects require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Version tool contracts and provide compatibility, migration, deprecation, and replay tests for changed parameters and results.

steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Safe AI agents and Python Record what required effort · Review Tool Schema Version, keep Tool Schema Version connected to executable Python model, interaction, tool, policy, state, approval, evaluation, security, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Version tool contracts and provide compatibility, migration, deprecation, and replay tests for changed parameters and results.
